steps.title: Require imagined and informed
steps.content: The student's claim has two parts: the film is not a precise historical retelling, but it is informed by research. Evidence must show both that Powers lacked detailed records of the meeting and that he researched the people involved.

steps.title: Test each quotation
steps.content:
• Choice A: Describes an early plan, not historical precision or research.
  • Choice B: Describes awards, not the film's relationship to history.
  • Choice C: Describes the film's theme but not whether it was imagined from research.
  • Choice D: Shows sparse historical details plus extensive research into the individuals.
The answer is D.
